ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for kimchi pancake; 'pancake food product' (FOODON_03315671) is the closest defensible parent class. Kimchi-specific preparation is not represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Pancakes) is the closest defensible match. No specific kimchi pancake entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for kimchi pancake or Korean savoury pancake as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Korean kimchi pancake is one medium pancake weighing approximately 150g, often served as a side dish or snack alongside rice and other banchan.

nutrient_source.title: Korean Kimchi Pancake (Kimchijeon) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Korean kimchi pancake (cr_id F05010003). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this entry at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for pancakes made with vegetable oil and egg (USDA SR Legacy #18259) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented as an adapted value.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Korean kimchi pancake (kimchijeon) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: wheat-flour savoury pancake / crêpe with mixed ingredients, which has a reported GI of approximately 66 in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The kimchi component (fermented vegetables) may modestly lower GI due to organic acids; however, the dominant wheat-flour batter base drives the GI estimate. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (65 × 25.9 × 1.5) / 100 = 25.2g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 516mg per 100g, primarily from fermented kimchi and kimchi brine
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid kimchi pancake due to its high sodium content of 516mg/100g; if consumed, use minimal kimchi brine in the batter, drain kimchi thoroughly, and avoid additional high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flour base contributing 25.9g carbohydrate/100g with only 2.1g dietary fibre and a medium-high GI of approximately 65
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should consume kimchi pancake in small portions due to its refined carbohydrate base (25.9g/100g, GI ~65) and high fat content (16.95g/100g) which together produce a high glycaemic load and may impair postprandial glucose control; substituting part of the wheat flour with whole-grain flour can reduce the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (16.95g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~2.8g/100g), estimated trans fat (~0.1g/100g from pan-frying), and high sodium (516mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit kimchi pancake due to its combination of high total fat, estimated trans fat from pan-frying, and high sodium (516mg/100g), all of which are associated with elevated cardiovascular risk; preparation with minimal oil in a non-stick pan at moderate heat can reduce fat and trans fat formation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated trans fat (~0.1g/100g from pan-frying) and sodium exceeding 400mg/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit kimchi pancake as the estimated trans fat from pan-frying is pro-inflammatory, sodium at 516mg/100g exceeds the 400mg/100g RA threshold worsening tissue swelling, and refined wheat flour contributes to glycaemic-load-driven inflammation; the fermented kimchi component provides some probiotic benefit but does not offset the overall inflammatory profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Korean kimchi pancake, known in Korean as 김치전 (kimchijeon) or 김치부침개 (kimchi buchimgae), is a traditional Korean savoury pan-fried pancake made by combining chopped fermented kimchi with a wheat flour and water batter, typically enriched with egg and green onion, then pan-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy on the outside while remaining soft and chewy inside. The dish has a distinctly tangy, spicy, and umami-rich flavour profile derived from the fermented cabbage kimchi, with a slightly crispy exterior and a moist, dense interior. It is commonly served as a banchan (side dish), snack, or anju (food paired with alcohol) in Korean cuisine, and is popular across all age groups. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 273 kcal, 4.1g protein, 16.95g fat, and 25.9g carbohydrate with 2.1g dietary fibre. The high sodium content of 516mg/100g from kimchi and brine makes it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The refined wheat flour base with a medium GI of approximately 65 and high glycaemic load makes portion control essential for people with type 2 diabetes. The estimated trans fat from pan-frying at high heat is a concern for those with rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease. The fermented kimchi component contributes probiotic bacteria and some anti-inflammatory compounds, which is a nutritional positive. This dish is not halal-certified in standard preparation due to potential cross-contamination and fermentation agents, and contains gluten and egg allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in kimchi pancake?
Is kimchi pancake high in sodium?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05010003) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat adapted from USDA SR Legacy closest analogue (pancake with vegetable oil and egg). GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (wheat-flour savoury pancake). Korean local name 김치전 is the standard, well-documented Korean term. All mandatory fields are populated.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.8g/100g) is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #18259 (pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe) as HPB Singapore Foodo does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. This is an approximate value. 2. Trans fat (0.1g/100g) is an estimate based on pan-frying at high heat with vegetable oil; no direct measurement is available from HPB or USDA for this specific preparation. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this Korean kimchi pancake preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour savoury pancake from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008); no direct GI measurement for kimchijeon exists in approved sources. 5. Halal status is marked false as standard kimchi preparation may use fish sauce or shrimp paste which are not universally halal-certified; consumers should verify with specific product/restaurant. 6. Vegetarian status is marked true for egg-and-flour based preparation without meat; vegan is false due to egg content.
